name: Gene Vincent
abstract: Vincent Eugene Craddock (February 11, 1935 – October 12, 1971), known as Gene Vincent, was an American rock and roll musician who pioneered the style of rockabilly. His 1956 top ten hit with his backing band the Blue Caps, "Be-Bop-a-Lula", is considered a significant early example of rockabilly.

about_quotes.speaker: Luis Conte
about_quotes.evidence: Gene Vincent. She had all these records of all these guys. So I grew up listening to the stuff in the house
about_quotes.doc_title: Luis Conte interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 24, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: James Hunter
about_quotes.evidence: Well when I was a teenager it was I think Eddie Cochran and Gene Vincent I got into.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Hunter interviewed by Monk Rowe, Boston, Massachusetts, July 25, 2018
